concerns.text: FR-002 requires calculating aggregated noise metrics (mean, median, 95th percentile) 'per day' for each grid cell. Task T013.5 explicitly instructs to 'average daily metrics into a single value per grid cell', which collapses the temporal dimension and violates the spec's requirement to retain daily aggregates. There is no task to implement the 'per day' aggregation logic required by FR-002.

FR-009 mandates applying Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction using 'spatially robust standard errors (e.g., Conley or cluster-robust SEs)'. Task T022 describes calculating Conley/Cluster-Robust SEs for 'all models' (including OLS, Lag, Error) but lacks the specific instruction to apply the FDR correction to the resulting p-values as required by FR-009. T023 applies FDR but does not explicitly link it to the robust SEs from T022, creating a gap in the traceability of the specific robust error estimation method required.

FR-010 and SC-004 mandate the pipeline must execute within 6 hours on 2 CPU cores with <7GB RAM for 50k cells. Task T036 is a generic 'performance optimization' placed in the final 'Polish' phase. There is no foundational task ensuring the architecture (e.g., chunking, memory management) is designed to meet these hard constraints during the initial implementation of FR-001/FR-002, risking an unscalable build.

The spec's Assumptions state that if a covariate is missing, the cell is 'excluded from model fitting' and the system will 'log a warning indicating the number of excluded cells'. Task T014 mentions 'handling missing covariates via exclusion (logging count)' but omits the explicit requirement to log a 'warning' level message, which is a specific behavioral requirement for traceability and debugging.

Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) requires that external datasets (including synthetic ones) be reproducible by re-running code against the same data. Task T005 requires generating synthetic data with 'stochastic spatial parameters' but does not explicitly include a task to 'pin' the random seed AND record the specific generated parameters in the artifact metadata/state file to ensure the exact same dataset is regenerated on every run, risking a violation of reproducibility.
